nodes.label: AI as writing assistant for drafting
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable for original writing without human input
nodes.grounding_span: I've found LLMs to be very good at summarizing, so often use them to draft up abstracts or conclusions

nodes.label: AI trustworthiness and verification
nodes.label_negative: AI inaccuracy and untrustworthiness for research tasks
nodes.grounding_span: If I can't trust the tools completely then I can't use them for research because I need to be sure my citations are correct

nodes.label: Firm boundary against AI in manuscript drafting to preserve human voice
nodes.grounding_span: I would love for AI to be able to write up certain sections... but ultimately the sections don't sound like they are written in my voice

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for proofreading and coding

nodes.label: Methodical experimentation and evaluation of tool capabilities
nodes.grounding_span: Once I tried them out I was able to identify fairly quickly the areas where they were more or less helpful

nodes.label: Verification through existing knowledge foundation
nodes.grounding_span: Because I know what I'm looking for it's easier to verify
